task_title: Provision Dev Infrastructure for Sigma-1 E2E Pipeline (Bolt - Kubernetes/Helm)
expansion_prompt: Break this task into subtasks covering: (1) Namespace creation with labels, (2) ExternalSecret CRD definitions for all 4 secrets, (3) Secret validation Job implementation with conditional logic for optional NOUS_API_KEY, (4) ConfigMap creation with service endpoints, (5) Health check verification and resource labeling. Consider that subtasks 1 and 2 can be parallel, subtask 3 depends on 2, and subtask 4 is independent. Include a documentation subtask for README on backing store paths.
reasoning: This task involves multiple Kubernetes resource types (namespace, ExternalSecrets, ConfigMap, Job), custom validation logic with conditional failure handling, cross-namespace service health checks, and documentation. Each resource type has distinct configuration and testing concerns, and the validation job requires scripting with nuanced error handling (optional vs required secrets).

task_title: Extend PM Server for Agent Delegation in Linear Issues (Nova - Bun/Elysia)
expansion_prompt: Break down into: (1) Audit and extend resolve_agent_delegates() mapping to cover 5+ agents, (2) Modify Linear issue creation flow to use delegate_id with agent:pending fallback, (3) Extend task schema with delegate_id and delegation_status fields, (4) Implement GET /api/delegation/status endpoint, (5) Implement GET /api/pipeline/status endpoint. Subtasks 1 and 3 can be done in parallel. Subtask 2 depends on both 1 and 3. Subtasks 4 and 5 are independent REST endpoints that can be built in parallel after the schema is defined.
reasoning: This involves auditing existing code, modifying a core creation flow with fallback logic, schema extensions, and two new REST endpoints. The delegation resolution has edge cases (null handling, label fallback) and requires integration with the Linear API using injected secrets. Multiple integration points and backward compatibility requirements add complexity.

task_title: Integrate Hermes Research into Deliberation Path (Nova - Bun/Elysia)
expansion_prompt: Break into: (1) Hermes endpoint discovery logic with service discovery fallback, (2) ResearchProvider abstraction with three concrete implementations (HermesProvider, NousProvider, SkipProvider), (3) Provider selection logic with circuit-breaker pattern and health check caching, (4) Integration into deliberation path - invoking provider and writing research memos, (5) Error handling and structured logging across all providers. Subtasks 1 and 2 can be parallel. Subtask 3 depends on 2. Subtask 4 depends on 1 and 3. Subtask 5 is cross-cutting.
reasoning: Three-tier fallback architecture with circuit-breaker pattern, health check caching, service discovery, timeout handling, and integration into an existing deliberation pipeline. The abstraction layer design, cascading fallback logic, and requirement that research never blocks the pipeline create significant complexity across multiple concerns.

task_title: Implement Design Snapshot PR Surfacing (Nova - Bun/Elysia)
expansion_prompt: Break into: (1) Investigate existing design snapshot mechanisms and create scaffold file generation logic, (2) Implement GitHub API integration for branch creation and PR submission using ExternalSecret token, (3) Define PR content structure with task scaffolds, design snapshots, and pipeline metadata, (4) Error handling for GitHub API failures with graceful degradation and PR URL storage in pipeline state. Subtask 1 can start independently. Subtask 2 is the core integration. Subtask 3 depends on 1. Subtask 4 is cross-cutting.
reasoning: Involves GitHub API integration (branch creation, file commits, PR creation), file generation for multiple scaffold types, structured metadata output, and error handling that must not crash the pipeline. The investigation of existing mechanisms adds uncertainty. Multiple file types and PR formatting requirements add moderate complexity.

task_title: Enable Discord and Linear Bridge Notifications (Nova - Bun/Elysia)
expansion_prompt: Break into: (1) NotificationService module with Discord bridge integration including payload format discovery and rich embeds, (2) Linear bridge notification integration with session references and PR links, (3) Pipeline lifecycle hooks wiring notifications to start/complete/error events with retry logic and structured logging. Subtask 1 and 2 are parallel. Subtask 3 depends on both.
reasoning: Requires integrating with two separate bridge services with potentially unknown payload formats, implementing retry logic, and hooking into the pipeline lifecycle at multiple points. The payload format discovery adds some uncertainty, but each integration follows a similar HTTP POST pattern. The graceful degradation requirement (notification failure must not block pipeline) adds design consideration.

task_title: Validate Task Generation with Agent Assignment (Nova - Bun/Elysia)
expansion_prompt: Break into: (1) Test PRD preparation with 5+ distinct agent types, (2) Pipeline execution orchestration through all 5 stages with stage-level validation checkpoints, (3) Linear API verification - confirming issues have correct assignees via direct API queries, (4) Validation report generation with all required fields and edge case tracking, (5) Expose GET /api/validation/report/{run_id} endpoint and pipeline state storage. Subtask 1 is independent. Subtask 2 depends on 1. Subtasks 3 and 4 depend on 2. Subtask 5 can be started early with mocked data.
reasoning: This is a full end-to-end orchestration task that exercises all previous tasks' outputs. It requires running a 5-stage pipeline, validating outputs at each stage, cross-referencing agent mappings with Linear API results, handling edge cases (rate limiting, mapping failures), and producing a structured validation report. The multiple validation checkpoints and the comprehensive report structure make this complex.

task_title: Implement Web Frontend for Delegation Status Dashboard (Blaze - React/Next.js)
expansion_prompt: Break into: (1) Next.js scaffold with Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and TypeScript configuration, (2) JWT authentication implementation with login page, middleware, and httpOnly cookie management, (3) Dashboard page with pipeline status banner, task table, status badges, and polling, (4) Pipeline run detail view at /dashboard/runs/{run_id} with validation report display, (5) Containerization with multi-stage Dockerfile and environment variable configuration. Subtasks 1 is prerequisite for 2-4. Subtasks 2, 3, and 4 can be partially parallelized after scaffolding. Subtask 5 is independent of UI features.
reasoning: Full Next.js application with authentication (JWT, httpOnly cookies, middleware), multiple pages, real-time polling, API integration with multiple endpoints, shadcn/ui component integration, error handling UX, and containerization. The auth system alone is a significant feature, and the dashboard has multiple data display patterns (tables, badges, banners, alerts).

task_title: End-to-End Pipeline Integration Test (Tess - Test frameworks)
expansion_prompt: Break into: (1) Test framework setup with environment configuration and timeout handling, (2) Mocking strategy implementation - decide and implement live vs mock for Linear, Discord, GitHub, and Hermes, (3) Pipeline completion test (AC-1) - trigger and validate full run, (4) Linear session and delegation tests (AC-2 + AC-3) - verify issues and assignees, (5) PR creation and Discord notification tests (AC-4 + AC-5), (6) Graceful degradation test and cleanup logic. Subtask 1 is prerequisite. Subtask 2 is prerequisite for 3-6. Subtasks 3-5 can run sequentially in one pipeline run but should be designed independently. Subtask 6 requires environment manipulation capability.
reasoning: This is a comprehensive integration test spanning all 5 acceptance criteria across multiple external services (Linear, GitHub, Discord, Hermes/NOUS). The mocking strategy decision is a significant design choice. Each AC requires different validation approaches (API queries, log inspection, state checks). The graceful degradation test requires environment manipulation. Cleanup of live resources adds operational complexity. 5-minute timeout and multi-service coordination make this architecturally complex.

task_title: Production Hardening: HA Scaling, CDN, TLS, Ingress, and Network Policies (Bolt - Kubernetes/Helm)
expansion_prompt: Break into: (1) HA scaling - replicas, PodDisruptionBudget, anti-affinity rules, and statelessness verification, (2) Ingress and TLS configuration with cert-manager and rate limiting, (3) Network policies - default deny plus explicit allow rules for all required traffic flows, (4) Resource management - requests, limits, and HPA configuration, (5) Health probes - liveness, readiness, and startup probe configuration, (6) CDN configuration for static assets if dashboard is deployed. Subtasks 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 can be largely parallelized as they affect different resource types. Subtask 6 depends on Task 7 deployment status. Network policies (subtask 3) require careful enumeration of all required traffic flows.
reasoning: Covers 6 distinct infrastructure concerns (HA, ingress/TLS, network policies, resource management, health probes, CDN), each requiring specific Kubernetes resource definitions and testing. Network policies in particular require careful enumeration of all inter-service and external communication paths. The HPA configuration requires understanding application behavior under load. Multiple distinct Kubernetes resource types and cross-cutting security concerns make this complex.

task_title: Production Hardening: RBAC, Secret Rotation, and Audit Logging (Bolt - Kubernetes/Helm)
expansion_prompt: Break into: (1) RBAC - ServiceAccount, Role with least-privilege permissions, RoleBinding, and Deployment update, (2) Secret rotation - ExternalSecret refreshInterval configuration and application-level rotation handling, (3) Rotation validation CronJob with alerting via Discord bridge, (4) Kubernetes audit policy configuration for sigma-1 namespace, (5) Application-level audit logging for all pipeline operations with log shipping, (6) Security scanning CronJob and SECURITY.md documentation. Subtasks 1, 4, and 6 are largely independent. Subtask 2 and 3 are related. Subtask 5 requires coordination with the application codebase. RBAC (subtask 1) has least-privilege design considerations that require careful analysis.
reasoning: Covers RBAC with least-privilege design, secret rotation with application-level handling, CronJob-based validation, Kubernetes audit policies, application audit logging, security scanning, and documentation. RBAC requires careful permission analysis. Secret rotation requires both infrastructure config and application behavior changes. Audit logging spans both Kubernetes and application layers. The security scanning and documentation add additional breadth. Multiple distinct security domains each with their own complexity.
